region_of_origin: Middle East / South Asia (served in Singapore)

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a generic kebab class (FOODON_03302051); no specific mutton sheekh kebab entry exists. Closest parent match used.
ontology.foodex2_description: Meat products — minced/ground meat preparations (grilled kebab analogue)
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a dedicated sheekh kebab code; A0FJP (minced meat preparation) is the closest defensible anchor for a grilled minced mutton product.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for mutton sheekh kebab as a specific food concept.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of Mutton Sheekh Kebab in Singapore is approximately 2 skewers weighing around 100g, often served with mint chutney, sliced onions, and flatbread or rice.

nutrient_source.title: Mutton Sheekh Kebab — HPB Singapore Foodo Nutrient Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Mutton Sheekh Kebab (item 147). All per_100g values are taken from this canonical entry with confidence 0.95. Saturated fat estimated at approximately 11g/100g based on typical mutton fat composition (USDA SR Legacy, mutton ground, cooked) as HPB does not separately report saturated fat for this item; documented as adapted for that field only.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson et al.; analogous estimate based on grilled minced meat with spices (low-carbohydrate meat dish)
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Mutton Sheekh Kebab. GI estimated at approximately 30 based on analogous low-carbohydrate grilled minced meat preparations from the University of Sydney GI Database. Carbohydrate content is 5.24g/100g; glycemic load per 100g serving = (30 × 5.24) / 100 = 1.57, rounded to 1.6. The low GI reflects the minimal carbohydrate content and the protein-fat matrix of the dish.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 1080mg/100g — far exceeds the 600mg/100g upper threshold for hypertension-safe foods
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid or strictly limit Mutton Sheekh Kebab due to its extremely high sodium content of 1080mg/100g; if consumed, request preparation with significantly reduced salt and avoid additional high-sodium condiments such as chutneys or sauces.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low carbohydrate (5.24g/100g) and low glycemic load (GL ~1.6) are favourable, but high fat content (26.4g/100g) may impair post-meal glucose regulation
type2_diabetes.guidance: Mutton Sheekh Kebab has a low glycemic load suitable for Type 2 diabetes management; however, the high fat content (26.4g/100g) may slow gastric emptying and increase post-meal glucose variability, so portion control and avoidance of high-GI accompaniments such as white rice or naan are recommended.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High total fat (26.4g/100g), estimated saturated fat (~11g/100g), trans fat (~0.2g/100g), cholesterol (85mg/100g), and sodium (1080mg/100g) collectively elevate cardiovascular risk
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should consume Mutton Sheekh Kebab only occasionally and in small portions, given its high saturated fat, trans fat, cholesterol, and sodium content, all of which contribute to atherogenic and hypertensive cardiovascular risk.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Mutton is rich in arachidonic acid (omega-6), driving TNF-alpha and IL-6 inflammatory pathways; high sodium (1080mg/100g) worsens tissue swelling; green chili is a nightshade vegetable
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid Mutton Sheekh Kebab due to its high arachidonic acid content from red meat, pro-inflammatory saturated and trans fats, and extreme sodium load; consider chicken or fish-based kebab alternatives.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Mutton Sheekh Kebab is a grilled minced mutton dish prepared by blending ground mutton with aromatic spices including cumin, coriander, garam masala, ginger, garlic, green chili, and onion, then moulding the seasoned mixture onto metal or bamboo skewers and grilling over charcoal or an open flame until cooked through and lightly charred. The dish originates from Middle Eastern and South Asian culinary traditions and is widely served in Singapore's hawker centres and Indian-Muslim restaurants. The texture is firm yet juicy, with a smoky, spiced flavour profile and a slightly crisp exterior. Nutritionally, each 100g provides 302 kcal, 10.7g protein, 26.4g fat (including approximately 11g saturated fat and 0.2g trans fat), 5.24g carbohydrate, 2.85g sugar, and 1080mg sodium. The high sodium content makes it unsuitable for individuals with hypertension. The high saturated fat, trans fat, and cholesterol (85mg/100g) elevate cardiovascular risk. Despite its low glycemic load (GL ~1.6), the high fat content may affect post-meal glucose variability in individuals with Type 2 diabetes. The dish is halal-certified in most Singapore establishments and is typically served with mint chutney, sliced raw onions, lemon wedges, and flatbread or rice.

confidence_reason: Core nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (item 147, Mutton Sheekh Kebab) with a confidence of 0.95. Saturated fat is adapted from USDA SR Legacy data for cooked ground mutton, documented as an adaptation. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database framework for low-carbohydrate grilled meat dishes.
notes: 1. monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractionation data for Mutton Sheekh Kebab not separately reported in HPB Singapore Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central for this specific preparation as of 2026. 2. trans_fat_g set to 0.2 based on typical trans fat content of cooked ground mutton (USDA SR Legacy); this is an adapted estimate, not a directly measured HPB value. 3. water_g set to -1: moisture content not reported in HPB Foodo for this item. 4. vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg set to -1: micronutrient detail not available in HPB Foodo for this dish entry as of 2026. 5. dietary_fibre_g is 0.0 as reported by HPB; this is a confirmed zero from the approved source reflecting the minimal plant-fibre content of the minced meat preparation. 6. Saturated fat (~11g/100g) is adapted from USDA SR Legacy (ground lamb/mutton, cooked) as HPB does not separately report saturated fat for this item. 7. GI of 30 is an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement for Mutton Sheekh Kebab exists in the University of Sydney GI Database. 8. Multilingual names provided for Singaporean context (English, Chinese, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) per G6 rules, as this dish is served in Singapore. Chinese names reflect the dish concept (grilled skewered mutton) rather than a direct transliteration, as no standard Chinese name exists for this specific dish in Singapore.
